data.description:
# GitHub Issues sync — provider parity with the Jira reconciler (de-Jira-ify the bridge)

## Context

Discovered from the export/import design brainstorm (`rule-swamp-pupil`, 2026-06-17). We want GitHub Issues sync at parity with the existing Jira sync: bidirectional, incremental, idempotent, dry-run by default.

**Critical design conclusion from that brainstorm:** GitHub sync must ride the EXISTING reconciler framework (binding store + outbound/inbound differs + applier), NOT the one-shot export/import path. Import is stateless / additive / lossy and would have to rebuild the entire binding/diff/conflict/convergence layer to do sync — the wrong substrate. git-bug's bridges confirm sync is bidirectional + incremental, distinct from bulk import. The one thing export/import leaves behind for this epic is the provider-neutral `--strip-external` association notion shipped by `rule-swamp-pupil`.

## What / Scope

The spine of this work is **generalizing the reconciler**, which is hardcoded to Jira in 6+ places:

- `jira_key` in the binding store (`rebar_reconciler/binding_store.py`) → a `{provider, external_key}` abstraction.
- `jira-` local-id prefix (`inbound_translate.py:105`).
- ADF (Atlassian Document Format) rich-text codec (`adf.py`) → a GitHub Markdown codec.
- per-type/status/priority maps (`outbound_differ.py`, `inbound_translate.py`) → per-provider registries.
- `<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->` loop-breaker marker (`inbound_differ.py`).
- `rebar-id:` Jira labels / entity properties used for binding recovery → a GitHub equivalent.

Then add a **GitHub provider**: REST/GraphQL client, auth (token / `gh`), pagination + rate-limit handling, field mapping (state↔status, labels↔tags, assignees, milestone↔?, comments with external-id dedup), and inbound/outbound apply leaves.

## Success Criteria

- A `{provider, external_key}` binding abstraction replaces hardcoded `jira_key`, with Jira refactored onto it and NO behavior regression (existing Jira reconciler tests stay green).
- GitHub provider does bidirectional incremental sync of issues (create/update/status/comments/labels), idempotent across passes, dry-run default, live writes gated by an env flag mirroring `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`.
- Conflict resolution + loop-breaker parity with Jira; `session_log` excluded (parity with `EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] reconciler binding/translate/differ/codec layers generalized to a provider abstraction; Jira migrated onto it with no regression (existing tests green)
- [ ] GitHub provider client: auth, pagination, rate-limit handling (read + write)
- [ ] field mapping GH↔rebar (state/labels/assignees/comments/external-id dedup) + Markdown codec
- [ ] bidirectional incremental sync + conflict resolution + loop-breaker; idempotent across passes
- [ ] dry-run default; live writes env-gated; `session_log` excluded
- [ ] tests against recorded GitHub fixtures + integration marker for live

Non-trivial; sized as a standalone epic. Builds on the provider-neutral external-association seam from `rule-swamp-pupil`.
data.title: GitHub Issues sync — provider parity with the Jira reconciler
